title: French Gender Agreement Mastery
description: Master articles, adjectives, past participles, and every gender agreement rule

crashCourse.title: Gender Agreement — The Complete System
crashCourse.subtitle: Every noun in French is masculine or feminine. This affects articles, adjectives, past participles, possessives, and demonstratives. Master the patterns below and you'll get it right every time.

sections.title: Step 1 — Learn the masculine endings
sections.content: Most nouns ending in these suffixes are masculine. Memorize the pattern, not each word.

examples.french: le garage, le courage, le village
examples.note: Exceptions: la page, la plage, la cage, l'image

examples.french: le bureau, le gâteau, le chapeau
le problème, le système, le thème

examples.note: Most -ée words are feminine, but these are traps

sections.title: Step 2 — Learn the feminine endings
sections.content: These endings almost always signal a feminine noun.

examples.french: la situation, la décision, la nation
la boulangerie, la vie, la pluie

sections.title: Step 3 — The tricky exceptions to memorize
sections.content: Some nouns break the pattern. These are the most common traps.
sections.tip: When in doubt, learn these exceptions by heart — they appear constantly in French.

sections.title: Step 4 — Adjective agreement rules
sections.content: French adjectives must agree in gender AND number with the noun they describe.
sections.tip: Default: add -e for feminine, -s for plural, -es for feminine plural. But many adjectives have special patterns.

examples.french: doux → douce, faux → fausse, fou → folle

sections.title: Step 5 — Past participle agreement
sections.content: This is the hardest part. Three rules determine when a past participle agrees.
sections.tip: Rule 1: With ÊTRE → agrees with subject. Rule 2: With AVOIR + preceding COD → agrees with COD. Rule 3: Reflexive → agrees with reflexive pronoun IF it's the COD.

examples.english: AVOIR + que (= les fleurs, f.pl.) before verb → achetées
AVOIR but COD after verb → NO agreement → acheté
Reflexive, but COD = les mains (after) → NO agreement → lavé
examples.note: Rule 3 (COD is les mains, not se)

sections.title: Step 6 — Possessives & demonstratives
sections.content: Possessives agree with the OWNED noun, not the owner. Demonstratives agree with the noun they point to.
sections.tip: mon/ton/son before feminine nouns starting with a vowel: mon amie (NOT ma amie). This avoids two vowel sounds clashing.

examples.english: école is feminine, but starts with vowel → son (not sa)
voiture is feminine → sa, regardless of whether the owner is male

examples.french: ce livre, cet homme, cette femme, ces enfants
examples.english: ce (m), cet (m before vowel/h), cette (f), ces (plural)

sections.title: Part 1: Le ou La? — Noun Gender
sections.instructions: Choose the correct article for each noun. Watch out for exceptions!

questions.explanation: Le musée — masculine despite the -ée ending. Words ending in -ée are usually feminine (la journée, l'idée), but musée is a famous exception.
La plage — feminine, even though -age is usually masculine (le garage, le courage). Plage, page, image, and cage are exceptions.

questions.sentence: Il y a ___ problème avec la commande.
questions.explanation: Un problème — masculine. All nouns ending in -ème are masculine: le thème, le système, le problème.

questions.sentence: ___ silence dans la salle est impressionnant.
questions.explanation: Le silence — masculine! Even though -ence is usually feminine (la patience, la violence), silence is a well-known exception.

questions.sentence: J'ai trouvé ___ image intéressante sur Internet.
questions.explanation: Une image — feminine. This is an exception to the -age = masculine pattern. L'image is always feminine.

questions.sentence: ___ lycée est près de la gare.
questions.explanation: Le lycée — masculine, another exception like le musée. Don't be tricked by the -ée ending.
La liberté — feminine. Nouns ending in -té/-ité are almost always feminine: la qualité, l'université, la beauté.
Une page — feminine. Like plage and image, this is an exception to the -age = masculine rule.
Le groupe — masculine, even though it ends in -e. Many common nouns ending in -e are masculine: le monde, le texte, le livre, le groupe.

questions.sentence: ___ eau de cette rivière est très claire.
questions.explanation: L'eau — feminine! Despite ending in -eau (usually masculine: le bureau, le gâteau), eau and peau are feminine exceptions. We use l' because it starts with a vowel.

sections.title: Part 2: Articles in Context
sections.instructions: Choose the correct article to complete each sentence. Pay attention to the gender of the noun.

questions.explanation: Une nouvelle voiture — voiture is feminine. The adjective nouvelle also confirms the feminine gender.

questions.sentence: Il mange ___ fromage tous les matins.
questions.explanation: Du fromage — fromage is masculine. The partitive article for masculine nouns is du (de + le).
De l'eau — eau is feminine but starts with a vowel, so we use de l' (not de la). This contraction happens with both masculine and feminine nouns starting with a vowel.

questions.sentence: ___ endroit est parfait pour la fête.
questions.explanation: L'endroit — endroit is masculine but starts with a vowel, so le becomes l'. You can tell it's masculine from: un endroit, cet endroit, un bon endroit.

questions.sentence: C'est ___ belle occasion de se retrouver.
questions.explanation: Une belle occasion — occasion is feminine (-sion ending = feminine). The adjective belle also confirms it.

questions.sentence: Je cherche ___ appartement près du centre.
questions.explanation: Un appartement — appartement is masculine (-ment ending = masculine). Despite starting with a vowel, the indefinite article stays un (not une).

questions.sentence: Il a ___ courage de parler en public.
questions.explanation: Le courage — courage is masculine (-age ending = masculine). We use le (not l') because courage starts with a consonant.
Une bonne journée — journée is feminine (-ée ending = feminine). Compare with le jour (masculine) — this is a common masculine/feminine pair.

sections.title: Part 3: Adjective Agreement
sections.instructions: Choose the correctly agreed form of the adjective.

questions.explanation: Grande — maison is feminine singular. Regular rule: add -e to make an adjective feminine.
Belles — fleurs is feminine plural. Beau → belle (feminine) → belles (feminine plural).
Dangereuse — situation is feminine singular. Pattern: -eux → -euse for feminine.
Sportive — Marie is feminine singular. Pattern: -if → -ive for feminine.
Bons — résultats is masculine plural. Bon → bons (just add -s for masculine plural).
Neuve — voiture is feminine singular. Neuf → neuve (for brand-new objects). Note: nouveau/nouvelle means 'new to you', neuf/neuve means 'brand new'.
Fausse — histoire is feminine singular. Faux → fausse is an irregular feminine form.
Travailleuses — étudiantes is feminine plural. Pattern: -eur → -euse for feminine, then add -s for plural.
Ouverte — porte is feminine singular. Regular rule: add -e to ouvert.

questions.sentence: Les enfants sont ___ de leur cadeau.
questions.explanation: Contents — enfants is masculine plural (or mixed group → masculine plural). When a group has both boys and girls, use masculine plural.

sections.title: Part 4: Irregular Adjectives
sections.instructions: These adjectives have special forms. Choose the correct one for the context.

questions.explanation: Bel — before a masculine noun starting with a vowel or mute h, beau becomes bel. Compare: un beau garçon, but un bel homme.
Vieille — ville is feminine singular. Vieux → vieille. (Vieil is only used before masculine nouns starting with a vowel: un vieil ami.)
Nouvelle — maison is feminine. Nouveau → nouvelle. (Nouvel is only before masculine nouns starting with a vowel: un nouvel appartement.)
Douce — mer is feminine singular. Doux → douce is irregular. (Doucement is an adverb, not an adjective.)
Folle — idée is feminine singular. Fou → folle. (Fol is used before masculine nouns starting with a vowel: un fol espoir.)
Vieil — imperméable is masculine and starts with a vowel sound. Before masculine vowel-initial nouns: vieux → vieil.
Gentille — femme is feminine singular. Gentil → gentille (double the l and add -e).
Nouvel — appartement is masculine and starts with a vowel. Before masculine vowel-initial nouns: nouveau → nouvel.

sections.title: Part 5: Past Participle Agreement
sections.instructions: Choose the correctly agreed past participle. Remember the three rules: être = agree with subject, avoir = agree with preceding COD, reflexive = agree if reflexive pronoun is COD.

questions.sentence: Marie est ___ au cinéma hier soir.
questions.explanation: Allée — with être, the past participle agrees with the subject. Marie is feminine singular → allée.
Parties — with être, agree with subject. Les filles is feminine plural → parties.

questions.sentence: La lettre que j'ai ___ est très longue.
questions.explanation: Écrite — with avoir, when the COD (que = la lettre, feminine singular) comes BEFORE the verb, the participle agrees with it.

questions.sentence: Elle s'est ___ les mains avant de manger.
questions.explanation: Lavé — no agreement! In 'se laver les mains', the COD is 'les mains' (which comes after the verb), not 'se'. The reflexive pronoun 'se' is the COI here. So: no agreement.

questions.sentence: Les fleurs que tu as ___ sont magnifiques.
questions.explanation: Achetées — with avoir, the COD (que = les fleurs, feminine plural) precedes the verb → agree. Feminine plural = -ées.

questions.sentence: Elles se sont ___ dans le miroir.
questions.explanation: Regardées — reflexive verb where 'se' is the COD (regarder quelqu'un). Se refers to elles (feminine plural) → agree.

questions.sentence: Ils sont ___ à Paris la semaine dernière.
questions.explanation: Allés — with être, agree with subject. Ils is masculine plural → allés.

questions.sentence: Les gâteaux que j'ai ___ étaient délicieux.
questions.explanation: Faits — with avoir, the COD (que = les gâteaux, masculine plural) precedes the verb → agree.
Devenue — devenir uses être, so agree with subject. Elle is feminine singular → devenue.

questions.sentence: La chanson qu'il a ___ était magnifique.
questions.explanation: Chantée — with avoir, the COD (que = la chanson, feminine singular) precedes the verb → agree.

sections.title: Part 6: Possessives & Demonstratives
sections.instructions: Choose the correct possessive or demonstrative form. Remember: possessives agree with the owned noun, not the owner!

questions.explanation: Mon amie — even though amie is feminine, we use mon because the word starts with a vowel. This avoids the awkward sound 'ma amie'. The rule: use mon/ton/son before ALL vowel-initial nouns, regardless of gender.
Son école — école is feminine but starts with a vowel → son (not sa). Same rule as mon amie.
Cette idée — idée is feminine → cette. (Cet is only for masculine nouns starting with a vowel.)
Cet homme — homme is masculine and starts with a vowel sound (the h is mute) → cet. This is the masculine equivalent of the vowel rule.
Ma nouvelle voiture — voiture is feminine and the next word (nouvelle) starts with a consonant, so we use ma. (If it were 'mon ancienne voiture', we'd use mon because ancienne starts with a vowel.)
Ces enfants — enfants is plural → ces. The demonstrative ces is used for all plural nouns regardless of gender.
Sa montre — montre is feminine and starts with a consonant → sa. Remember: the possessive agrees with montre (feminine), not with elle.
Cet appartement — appartement is masculine and starts with a vowel → cet.

sections.title: Part 7: Spot the Gender Error
sections.instructions: Each sentence has a gender agreement error. Find it and type the corrected full sentence.

questions.sentence: La musée est fermé le lundi.
questions.correct: Le musée est fermé le lundi.

questions.sentence: J'ai trouvé une bon endroit pour la fête.
questions.correct: J'ai trouvé un bon endroit pour la fête.
questions.hint: Check the article and adjective for endroit.
questions.errorType: une bon → un bon (endroit is masculine)

questions.sentence: La petite oiseau chante dans le jardin.
questions.correct: Le petit oiseau chante dans le jardin.
questions.errorType: La petite → Le petit (oiseau is masculine)

questions.hint: Words ending in -ème are usually which gender?
The adjective must agree with voiture.
With être, the past participle agrees with the subject.

questions.sentence: Son anniversaire est la semaine prochain.
questions.correct: Son anniversaire est la semaine prochaine.
questions.hint: The adjective must agree with semaine.
Hôtel is masculine. Which demonstrative do we use before a masculine noun starting with a vowel sound?
questions.errorType: Cette → Cet (hôtel is masculine, starts with vowel sound)

questions.sentence: J'ai mangé une bon repas ce soir.
questions.correct: J'ai mangé un bon repas ce soir.
questions.errorType: une bon → un bon (repas is masculine)

questions.sentence: Les enfants sont contentes de leur cadeau.
questions.correct: Les enfants sont contents de leur cadeau.
questions.hint: For a mixed group, which gender do we use?

sections.title: Part 8: Masculin ou Féminin?
sections.instructions: Classify each noun as masculine or feminine.

questions.sentence: L'occasion ne se présente qu'une fois.
Le groupe part à huit heures.

sections.title: Part 9: Translate with Correct Agreement
sections.instructions: Translate each sentence into French. Pay careful attention to gender agreement on articles, adjectives, and past participles.

questions.english: The house is big and beautiful.
questions.correct: La maison est grande et belle.
questions.hint: Maison is feminine. Both adjectives must agree.
alternates: La maison est grande et belle

questions.hint: Aller uses être → past participle agrees with subject (elle).
Amie starts with a vowel → use mon (not ma). Intelligente agrees with amie.
Ville is feminine. Old → vieille (feminine of vieux). Beautiful → belle.

questions.english: The flowers that I bought are red.
questions.correct: Les fleurs que j'ai achetées sont rouges.
questions.hint: Avoir + preceding COD (que = les fleurs, f.pl.) → achetées. Rouge → rouges (plural).
alternates: Les fleurs que j'ai achetées sont rouges

questions.hint: Voiture is feminine → sa (not son). Nouveau → nouvelle before feminine noun.
Idée is feminine → cette (not ce/cet). Intéressant → intéressante.

questions.english: The children are happy and excited.
questions.correct: Les enfants sont contents et excités.
questions.hint: Enfants (mixed/default) is masculine plural. Both adjectives take masculine plural form.
alternates: Les enfants sont contents et excités
Les enfants sont heureux et excités
